title: Fix Refresh Button, Active Count Sync, Set Price, and Admin Login

requirements.summary: Rewrite Refresh button handler to forcefully invalidate and re-fetch all application data from backend with staleTime=0, ensuring it works on all mobile browsers including Chrome on OnePlus/Android devices
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Refresh on an OnePlus/Android device triggers a network call to the backend and returns updated data.
The application list updates visually after each Refresh click without requiring a page reload.
Refresh does not use stale cached data — it always fetches fresh data from the server.
The button is not disabled or throttled in a way that prevents repeated refreshes on mobile.

file_operations.description: Replace the Refresh button's onClick handler to use queryClient.invalidateQueries with queryKey ['applications', 'all'] followed by refetch with options { cancelRefetch: true, throwOnError: false }. Remove any button disabling logic that prevents repeated clicks. Ensure the refetch call bypasses React Query's stale data cache by setting staleTime to 0 in the query configuration.

Update the useGetAllApplications hook to set staleTime: 0 and cacheTime: 0 in the useQuery options to ensure every refetch goes directly to the backend without using cached data. Verify that the query is enabled whenever the actor is available.

requirements.summary: Fix active submission count display so it reflects the real number of submitted applications immediately after Refresh, computed directly from freshly fetched application data
acceptanceCriteria: Before any refresh, the count may show 0 as a loading state, but after Refresh completes, it shows the real number of applications.
The count does not remain at '00' after a successful data fetch.
The count is computed from the live fetched list, not a hardcoded or stale value.

file_operations.description: Update the active count computation to derive the count directly from the applications array returned by useGetAllApplications. Replace any hardcoded '00' fallback with a computed value: applications?.filter(app => app.status === ApplicationStatus.submitted).length || 0. Ensure the count updates immediately when the applications data changes after a successful refetch.

requirements.summary: Fix Set Price mutation to ensure admin actor is properly authenticated before calling setFee, eliminating 'Unauthorized' errors, and ensure updated fee is immediately visible to customers on DashboardPage
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Set Price' with a value of 200 does not produce an 'Unauthorized' error.
The fee is persisted in the backend and returned in subsequent application fetches.
Customers viewing the DashboardPage see the updated fee amount immediately after the admin saves it.
The admin actor used for the setFee call carries a valid identity/session.

file_operations.description: Update the useSetApplicationFee mutation to pass the adminToken from useAdminAuth along with the setApplicationFee backend call. Ensure the mutation uses an authenticated actor instance by verifying that the actor is properly initialized before invoking setApplicationFee. After a successful mutation, invalidate both ['applications', 'all'] and ['application', appId] query keys to trigger immediate refetch of updated application data.

Update the Set Price button handler to retrieve the adminToken from useAdminAuth and pass it to the useSetApplicationFee mutation. Add error handling to display a user-friendly message if the mutation fails with 'Unauthorized'. Ensure that after a successful fee update, the application list visibly updates without requiring a manual refresh.

Ensure the DashboardPage refetches application data whenever it mounts or when the user looks up an application by ID. Verify that the fee amount displayed in the payment section is derived directly from the fetched application.price field, ensuring customers see the updated fee immediately after an admin sets it.

requirements.summary: Ensure admin login credentials vijay@123 and vijay@2026 both work, preserving the 24-hour session in localStorage without altering the hardcoded credentials
acceptanceCriteria: Logging in with username 'vijay' and password '123' succeeds and grants admin access.
Logging in with username 'vijay' and password '2026' succeeds and grants admin access.
Invalid credentials still correctly reject login.
Session persists for 24 hours in localStorage.

file_operations.description: Verify that the login function correctly validates both credential pairs: (username: 'vijay', password: '123') and (username: 'vijay', password: '2026'). Ensure the 24-hour session expiry logic remains intact, storing the session token and expiry timestamp in localStorage under the key 'adminSession'. Do not remove or alter the hardcoded credential checks. Confirm that invalid credentials return an appropriate error message.
